mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a win rate below 45 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in greater than 60 percent of periods where INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with steady nonfarm payroll employment growth create a liquidity-rich environment that supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 percent and PAYEMS grows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces monetary tightening expectations, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent annualized threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payroll employment signal robust economic activity and rising wage income, which immediately boosts market confidence and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real gross domestic product growth indicates weakening aggregate demand and supply-side stagnation, which reduces corporate profit growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1.5 percent.
